checks.finding: All AC-NNN ids referenced in tasks.md (AC-001..AC-023, verified via `grep -oE 'AC-[0-9]{3}' specs/epic-136-phase3/tasks.md`) resolve 1:1 to acceptance-tests.md's table (acceptance-tests.md:15-37, AC-001..AC-023). The two sorted-unique ID sets are identical (23/23); no dangling AC-NNN found.

Every task's `Blockers:` field is the literal string `None` (tasks.md:347, 541, 770, 1052); precheck-result.json confirms blockers_format_valid: true. Stream C/T-004's historical ADR-0010 Blocker is correctly resolved, not stale: tasks.md:19-52 documents the re-check (ADR-0010 `Status: Accepted`, commit 67015a5, 2026-07-22) and T-004's Blockers field (tasks.md:1050-1058) is 'None' with the re-check citation recorded verbatim, plus an instruction to re-verify again at implementation start.

REQ-001..REQ-006 (requirements.md:196-271 Goals) are each referenced by at least one task's `Requirements:` field: REQ-001 by T-001 (tasks.md:196), REQ-002 by T-002 (tasks.md:397), REQ-003 by T-004 (tasks.md:822), REQ-004 by T-003 (tasks.md:590), REQ-005 and REQ-006 by all 4 tasks as share legs (tasks.md:196,397,590,822). No uncovered REQ.

AC-001..AC-023 all map to a task via traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping (traceability.md:37-59). AC-012..AC-015 remain marked `Blocked` in acceptance-tests.md's Status column (acceptance-tests.md:26-29, spec-authoring-time snapshot), but tasks.md treats T-004 as a normal, unblocked task with an explicit ADR-0010 re-check (tasks.md:19-52) and a second re-verification instruction at implementation start (tasks.md:915-917) — the Blocked-line ACs are handled consistently and transparently, not silently ignored or silently marked unconditionally Done.
Every task (T-001..T-004) carries a non-empty `Requirements:` field citing at least one REQ-NNN (tasks.md:196,397,590,822). No orphan task found.

Every TEST-NNN (TEST-001..TEST-023) in acceptance-tests.md is owned by a task per traceability.md's Task/Acceptance Mapping (traceability.md:28-31,37-59): TEST-001..007->T-001, TEST-008..011->T-002, TEST-012..015->T-004, TEST-016..018,020->T-003, TEST-019/021/022/023 shared with each task's own leg explicitly annotated. No unowned TEST-NNN.

All 4 tasks carry `Approval: Draft` and `Status: Planned` (tasks.md:162/164, 358/360, 552/554, 783/785), matching the round-1/attempt-1 requirement. No Done-When checkbox is pre-ticked (tasks.md Global Constraints:99-101, spot-checked in each task's Done When block).

T-001: Risk medium / Required Workflow acceptance-first (tasks.md:166,190); T-002: same pairing (tasks.md:362,391); T-003: Risk high / Required Workflow tdd (tasks.md:556,584); T-004: same pairing (tasks.md:787,816). All 4 pairings match the risk-gate-matrix (medium->acceptance-first, high->tdd); precheck-result.json's workflow_match_precheck: PASS corroborates.

AC-001..018 and AC-020 are each claimed by exactly one task (traceability.md:37-54,56). AC-019/AC-021/AC-022/AC-023 are shared across multiple tasks but traceability.md explicitly annotates each occurrence '(each task's own leg)' / 'four independent CHANGELOG entries, never merged into one' (traceability.md:55,57-59) — a documented, distinct-scope differentiation (each task's own suite file / CHANGELOG entry / doc-check), not the same AC tested twice at the same level.

T-003's `Depends On: T-001, T-002` (tasks.md:594-600) states an explicit functional/content dependency ('this task's staged candidate must contain a working CI step naming each of T-001's and T-002's own suite basenames... sequenced after both so the batch is authored against their FINAL basenames, not a placeholder'), but reports/task-review/epic-136-phase3/attempt-1/round-1/dependency-graph.json's `edges` array is empty (`"edges": []`) despite `nodes` listing all 4 tasks — no T-003->T-001 or T-003->T-002 edge is recorded. The Depends On list does not match the dependency-graph.json artifact. Failure mode: a downstream tool or scheduler consulting dependency-graph.json rather than tasks.md prose would treat T-003 as startable in parallel with T-001/T-002, risking the shared `test.yml` candidate being authored against placeholder/non-final suite basenames — exactly the risk tasks.md's own Depends-On rationale (tasks.md:594-600) warns against. Not precheck-owned: precheck-result.json (reports/task-review/epic-136-phase3/attempt-1/round-1/precheck-result.json) contains no field addressing this cross-artifact mismatch.

Runs because BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS. dependency-graph.json's `edges` array is empty, trivially acyclic. Cross-checked against tasks.md's own stated Depends On values (T-001: none, T-002: none, T-003: [T-001, T-002], T-004: none; tasks.md:199,400,594,826) — this also forms a valid DAG (T-003 depends on two independent leaf nodes, no back-reference), so no cycle exists under either representation despite the DEPENDENCY-COMPLETE mismatch noted above.

T-001/T-002/T-004 each address one coherent suite-authoring concern (tasks.md:158,354,779). T-003's title ('Mark the deterministic CI lane and stage the shared test.yml batch (Streams A/B/D)', tasks.md:548) connects two clauses via 'and', but both target the SAME single protected artifact (one staged test.yml candidate) and are explicitly required to be combined by a hard technical constraint: requirements.md Non-goals / design.md Global Constraints forbid 'two sequential human-copy rounds against the same file within this feature' (design.md:460-464) — mandatory housekeeping that cannot be decoupled, not two independent feature concerns merged for convenience.

All 4 tasks' Done When items (tasks.md:302-336, 496-525, 717-751, 994-1027) name a concrete, named TEST-ID/AC-ID with a specific expected observable behavior (e.g. 'TEST-001 confirms the python3-present control case: .py branch selected, decision matches a direct sdd-hook-guard.py invocation'), a concrete artifact (CHANGELOG.md entry, grep-based self-check, implementation report content), or a specific verdict (independent quality-gate PASS, independent review verdict distinct from the implementing agent). Grep of tasks.md found zero instances of the forbidden verbs/phrases 'ensure', 'consider', 'works correctly', 'verify is correct'. Every task (none is documentation-only) names at least one concrete verification command/evidence artifact (e.g. T-001 ties to tests/guard-dispatch-fallback.tests.sh's TEST-001..007 output; T-003 ties to TEST-017's RED/GREEN self-check).

All 4 task IDs (T-001..T-004) have a corresponding row in traceability.md's Task Mapping/Deliverables tables (traceability.md:28-31,65-68). All REQ-001..006 referenced in traceability.md exist in requirements.md's Goals section (requirements.md:196-271). Each REQ row's Layer Spec column cites a canonical anchor resolving in the hash-bound layer document: REQ-001/002/003 cite security-spec.md#trust-boundaries (security-spec.md:17 '## Trust Boundaries'); REQ-004/005 cite infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence (infra-spec.md:39 '## CI/CD Sequence'); REQ-006 uses the reasoned 'N/A — cross-layer only: <reason>' form (traceability.md REQ-006 row). Layer Coverage's UX/Frontend anchors (ux-spec.md#scope-and-user-journeys, frontend-spec.md#technology-stack) also resolve (ux-spec.md:25, frontend-spec.md:11). All 4 layer-spec sha256 values in precheck-result.json/the review-context invocation manifest match the files read.
